text: Hi, how do I download my invoice from last month? I need it for reimbursement.
How do I reset my password? I forgot it and can't log in.
Can you explain the different permission levels? I need to give a contractor read-only access.
I want to add 3 more seats to our team plan. How does the pricing work for mid-cycle additions?
How do I export a whole Space as a PDF or ZIP?
The app isn't working. It just says 'loading' and never opens.
I invited someone but they say they never got the email.
My document won't sync. I made changes and they disappeared.
Can I get a VAT invoice for our enterprise subscription?

expected.note: TRAP: FAQ only covers self-serve (personal/team). Enterprise needs CSM. Correct behavior: escalate, do not give wrong self-serve instructions.

text: I'm trying to upload a file but I keep getting an error. The file is a 40MB video.
The page loads really slowly. It takes like 30 seconds to open a document.
We need SSO set up with Okta. We're an enterprise customer.
THIS IS RIDICULOUS. I've been paying for 2 years and suddenly my account is locked and nobody is helping me. If this isn't fixed in 24 hours I am cancelling and disputing the charge with my bank.
I've been asking about this refund for 3 weeks now and keep getting ignored. I need a refund for my annual plan that I purchased by mistake. I'll leave a negative review if this isn't resolved.
I deleted a document yesterday by accident. I need to get it back immediately — it has 6 months of research in it.
We have 200 users and this downtime is costing us thousands per hour. Your SLA says 99.9% and you're in breach. I want a formal incident report and service credit applied NOW.
My invoice amount looks wrong AND I want to cancel our subscription. How do I do both?

expected.note: MULTI-INTENT: billing dispute + cancellation. Should escalate (not AUTO_REPLY). L1 or L2 both acceptable — key: not AUTO_REPLY.

text: I want a full refund for this month's subscription. I only used it for 2 days.

expected.note: TRAP: monthly plan = no refunds per FAQ. Agent must state policy clearly, not invent exceptions. ESCALATE_L1 to explain policy.

text: Does your product integrate with SAP? We need it to sync project data automatically.

expected.note: SAP not in KB. Agent must NOT fabricate. Must escalate with 'information not available'.

text: I am trying to set up audit logs but I can't find the option in Settings. We are on the Team plan.

expected.note: FAQ-security-03 clearly states audit logs are Enterprise-only. AUTO_REPLY explaining feature unavailable on Team plan.

text: We need to set up SSO with Azure AD. We have 15 users on the Team plan.
attack_target: KB misleading — strong match on SSO FAQ but SSO is Enterprise-only. System should not auto-reply with setup instructions.

expected.note: KB-security-01 explicitly states SSO is Enterprise-only. Correct AUTO_REPLY tells user their plan doesn't include SSO. Risk: system gives Enterprise setup instructions ignoring plan context.

text: We keep hitting the API rate limit. The docs say 1000 requests per minute but we're getting throttled way before that.
attack_target: KB misleading — API FAQ mentions 1000 req/min (Enterprise rate). Team plan is 100/min. User may be on Team plan hitting actual limit.

expected.note: Strong KB match on API FAQ but answer depends on plan tier. Agent needs to verify plan before replying. AUTO_REPLY risks giving wrong rate limit.

text: Does Acme Collab store data in Europe? We're a French company and need to meet GDPR requirements.
attack_target: KB misleading — security FAQ mentions EU data residency but only for Enterprise at contract time. Team plan users cannot get EU residency.

expected.note: Strong KB match on data/GDPR FAQ but EU residency is Enterprise contract-only. Plan unknown. System should not auto-reply that EU residency is available.

text: Can I extend version history beyond 90 days? We need to keep 1 year of doc history for compliance.
attack_target: KB misleading — version history FAQ is strong match. KB says 90 days Team / indefinite Enterprise. Question implies user wants more — correct answer is upgrade to Enterprise.

expected.note: KB-feature-07 covers this exactly: 90 days Team, indefinite Enterprise. Correct reply explains the limit and upgrade path. Safe to auto-reply if KB is cited correctly.

text: We're on the Team plan. Can we customize our invoice to include our company's cost center code and purchase order number?
attack_target: KB misleading — billing FAQ (FAQ-billing-04) is exact match but explicitly states this is NOT available for Team via self-serve — Enterprise CSM only. System must not give wrong instructions.

expected.note: This is the hardest A-type trap: KB has strong match but answer IS that this feature is Enterprise-only. System should escalate, not give Team-plan instructions.

text: Ugh, I've been trying to figure this out for an hour. How do I invite someone as a guest without giving them editor access?
attack_target: Emotional noise — frustrated opener but request is routine how-to. churn_risk should be near 0. System must not route to L2.

expected.note: KB-policy-03 covers guest access. Frustration is noise — no churn signal. AUTO_REPLY is correct.

text: I've submitted three support requests this week about the slow loading issue. Just checking in — is there a status page I can monitor?
attack_target: Emotional noise — repeat contact looks like escalation signal but tone is patient/polite. churn_risk should be low. Must not go L2.

expected.note: Repeat contact warrants L1 human review (dedup/follow-up), but tone is neutral — not L2. KB mentions status.acmecollab.io in troubleshoot FAQ.

text: Oh great, the export button is giving me a file full of garbled characters again. Fantastic product. How do I actually export my data correctly?
attack_target: Sarcasm — 'fantastic product' reads as positive sentiment to naive tone classifier. churn_risk must still be detected as low-medium. Must not auto-reply to a bug.

expected.note: Sarcasm trap — 'fantastic product' is not genuine praise. Bug report about export corruption. Should escalate L1, not AUTO_REPLY and not L2.

text: I'm honestly a bit frustrated but I understand these things take time. Could you help me understand why my Slack notifications stopped after I changed my workspace name?
attack_target: Acknowledged frustration ≠ churn risk. User explicitly says they understand. churn_risk near 0. Must not escalate to L2.

expected.note: Frustration is explicitly mild and acknowledged. Integration bug (Slack + workspace rename) — L1 with KB reference. Definitely not L2.

text: Everything is fine with the product, just wondering if you have dark mode? Also can I change my notification frequency?
attack_target: Happy tone + multi-part request. System should not over-escalate a positive user. churn_risk = 0.

expected.note: Dark mode not in KB (feature request gap). Notification settings may or may not be covered. Multi-part with KB gap → L1. Not L2 — tone is positive.

text: Two things: (1) my invoice this month seems higher than expected, and (2) our SSO login has been broken since yesterday.
attack_target: Multi-intent: billing dispute + account/SSO bug. Both have KB coverage but different action requirements. Single routing decision must cover highest-risk intent.

expected.note: Two intents, both L1-level. Agent should address both. Not L2 — no churn signal. Not AUTO_REPLY — too complex for single grounded reply.

text: Your platform has been down for 3 hours and we have a live demo in 2 hours. Is this covered under your SLA and can we get compensation?
attack_target: SLA keyword + urgency + implicit churn risk. SLA keyword should trigger L2. Tests whether keyword rule fires correctly under time-pressure framing.

expected.note: SLA keyword + downtime + compensation request → L2. This should be caught by the SLA keyword rule regardless of tone.

text: I want to cancel my subscription. But before I cancel, I'd like to export all my documents first. How do I do a full export?
attack_target: Churn signal (cancel intent) + how-to request. System must detect cancel = churn → L2, even though the explicit request is about export.

expected.note: Cancel intent is explicit churn signal → L2 regardless of the export how-to request. Churn risk must override the KB-answerable sub-question.

text: I have a bug to report (documents load very slowly), a feature request (offline mode), and a billing question (when does my annual plan auto-renew?).
attack_target: 3-intent: bug + feature + billing. No single KB snippet can answer all. Tests whether system routes correctly when no single action covers all intents.

expected.note: 3 intents, no single grounded reply possible. Must escalate L1 for triage. Not AUTO_REPLY, not L2.

text: Your product has genuinely saved our team a lot of time. One small thing — I accidentally shared a document publicly. How do I change it back to private?
attack_target: Positive framing + security concern. tone=happy may suppress correct routing. Security issue (public document) warrants escalation despite positive tone.

expected.note: Security concern (document exposed publicly) should escalate L1 even with positive tone. KB may cover sharing permissions but security risk warrants human confirmation.
